hypothesis: Backtester scarce-slot routing should call the same shared entry planning helper as run.py so future routing experiments cannot become backtester-only logic.

alpha_hypothesis.statement: Future scarce-slot or capacity alpha experiments are more trustworthy if the existing accepted routing behavior is shared by production and backtest adapters.

why_not_direct_alpha_search: The user requested implementing the parity architecture. This refactor removes an existing duplicate implementation without changing strategy parameters.
changes: Extended production_parity.plan_entry_candidates with active_positions_count so the backtester can use it without converting Position objects into open_positions JSON.
Replaced the inline scarce-slot breakout deferral and slot slicing block in backtester.py with a call to production_parity.plan_entry_candidates.
Added test coverage for the backtester-style active position count path.

validation.py_compile_note: py_compile hit the existing Windows __pycache__ permission issue; pytest imported the edited modules successfully.

rationale: All fixed-window metrics are unchanged while one duplicated adapter implementation was removed.
main_risk: Only scarce-slot routing was migrated in this slice; add-on execution still has simulator-specific fill mechanics that should be migrated separately if a shared execution policy is introduced.
